regimeSub: Warsh Fed hawkish hold meets soft June payrolls (57K) as CPI sticky at 4.2%; Trump tears up Iran ceasefire.
themes: Fed Chair Warsh at ECB Sintra vowed to 'disappoint' inflation doves, July FOMC minutes showed split on hikes → UST 10Y elevated at 4.54%, real rates compressing risk premia
June NFP printed 57K vs consensus miss, unemployment 4.2%, labor force participation at 5-year low → stagflation fears rise as growth softens while inflation persists at 4.2%
Trump ripped up Iran ceasefire July 9 and ordered Spain trade cutoff → EuroStoxx plunged 1.6%, geopolitical risk premium repricing despite Brent normalizing to $79 on OPEC+ output expansion
BOK signaled end to easing cycle citing FX stability and inflation concerns → USDKRW sticky at 1505, KOSPI outperformance (7072) driven by SK Hynix HBM dominance over Samsung

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: 10Y 4.54% minus 4.2% CPI = ~34bp real; compressed vs H1 but hawkish Warsh keeps floor under nominals

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: 2Y 4.16% / 10Y 4.54% = +38bp steepener; term premium rebuilding on fiscal concerns and inflation persistence

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Spreads contained despite geopolitical noise; VIX at 15 signals complacency vs stagflation backdrop

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 15.03 subdued despite Trump Iran ceasefire collapse; vol sellers in control but headline risk elevated

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $79.27 back to pre-Iran war levels per Reuters; OPEC+ July output expansion offsets Hormuz reopening hedges

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $4078 recovering from worst monthly drop since 2008 in June; real rate compression and geopolitics bid

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1505 range-bound as BOK hawkish hold supports won; JPY weakness (162) keeps JPYKRW cross at 9.29

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: SK Hynix overtook Samsung on HBM AI demand; Citi hiking TSMC PT 30%+ ahead of earnings

headline: Warsh's hawkish Fed hold collides with soft June payrolls and Trump's Iran ceasefire collapse, leaving markets in stagflationary limbo with VIX deceptively calm at 15.
